title: Hyper-V Administrators should not require admin
body:
### Issue Description

Podman checks for admin permissions when running with Hyper-V on Windows. Members of the group `Hyper-V Administrators` should be able to manage VMs without admin permissions.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run `podman machine init` without admin privileges.

### Describe the results you received

Error about missing permissions.

### Describe the results you expected

It should work.

### podman info output

```yaml
OS: windows/amd64
provider: hyperv
version: 5.1.2
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Windows 11

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: hyperv does not technically require admin but the use of the registry keys to create the vsock does.
would people rather have a priv elevation for `podman machine init`?

> hyperv does not technically require admin but the use of the registry keys to create the vsock does.

It should still work for members of `Hyper-V Administrators`, depending on how podman does it. I'll have a look at the code later.

> would people rather have a priv elevation for `podman machine init`?

If admin is a requirement, this is probably the preferred way in restricted enterprise environments.

> It should still work for members of `Hyper-V Administrators`, depending on how podman does it. I'll have a look at the code later.

When I tested this last time, this was not true.  The hvsock registry entries required admin.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Throwing my .02 in here.

I use Multipass and it does a similar setup to Podman and **does not** need admin to run, but uses a 'daemon' to do the admin required stuff.  Not sure if this helps, but seems Podman for Windows could do similar.

For WSL, seems odd you'd need admin as well, do you?

Now that https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26277 has been merged, the first `machine init` needs to be run in an admin terminal, but consequent `machine init` don't need it, as well as other machine commands as `start`, `stop`, `ls`.... Belonging to the hyperv administrators group is the only pre-requisite.

The first machine init requires to be run in an admin terminal because we need to write in the Windows registry HKLM to create the Hyper-V sockets ([that's required for host/guest communications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/virtualization/hyper-v/make-integration-service)).

Has #26277 landed in Podman 5.8.0?

I just upgraded to Podman 5.8.0 and deleted and recreated my Podman machine. However I still cannot run `podman machine` commands without elevated privileges even though my account is a member of the Hyper-V Administrators group.

Note that I am using a network (work) account to log on to my laptop. I do not use a local account. Not sure if this matters.

```
podman --version
podman version 5.8.0

podman machine start --log-level debug
time="2026-02-13T13:45:47+01:00" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2026-02-13T13:45:47+01:00" level=debug msg="Using Podman machine with `hyperv` virtualization provider"
Error: hyperv machines require admin authority
time="2026-02-13T13:45:47+01:00" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
```

@rveerd, the commits in #26277 haven't been ported to the branch v5.8. `v5.8` is based on `v5.7`, not `main`. It contains only a few selected commits backported from the `main` branch. Apologies for the confusion.
